subject: red pandas
one_liner: A longform publishing home for red pandas, who have a great deal to say and roughly four waking hours to say it.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan trade-publication confidence. Treats red panda biology (an 18-hour sleep cycle, a solitary disposition, a diet of bamboo) as serious editorial market constraints, never as a joke the brand is in on. Sells a reading platform to an animal that will not read it, with total sincerity.

hero.h1: Publishing for the solitary and arboreal.
hero.sub: Frond is where red pandas serialize their thinking between naps. One essay at a time, from the canopy, to an audience of no one in particular.

features.title: Naps, not minutes
features.blurb: Every essay is measured in naps to finish, never minutes to read. A nine-nap think piece sets honest expectations for a reader who is, statistically, already asleep.

features.title: Claps settle in bamboo
features.blurb: Readers reward your work in claps, which clear to bamboo each night at a floating rate we set and decline to explain. Withdraw to any hollow log within three business seasons.

features.title: Solitary by default
features.blurb: Comments ship disabled and stay that way. Red pandas do not co-author, do not reply, and do not want notes. The discussion rail is left empty on purpose, as a courtesy.

pricing_tiers.name: Apex Frugivore
perks: First refusal on the homepage frond

testimonials.quote: I published one essay about a single bamboo shoot. It now supports my entire extended family across two valleys.
testimonials.name: Pemba Sett
testimonials.title: Senior Essayist, Eastern Himalaya Desk

testimonials.quote: The badge promised three naps. It took five. I have never in my life felt more accurately measured.
testimonials.name: Dorje Vale
testimonials.title: Contributing Frugivore at Large

testimonials.quote: Solitary Mode means not one creature has ever finished a thing I wrote. This is, and I cannot stress this enough, the dream.
testimonials.name: Anjali Frond
testimonials.title: Reclusive Columnist & Long-Term Tree Tenant

demo_screen_concept: A Medium-style longform reader feed, set as a centered single column of red panda essays. Each essay card carries an author avatar, a title, a one-line dek, and an 'est. naps to finish' badge in place of a read-time. A floating clap button you tap to increment claps, with a live bamboo-balance figure beside it that converts claps to bamboo at a posted floating rate. A 'Solitary Mode' toggle in the header grays out an already-empty comments rail. One member-only essay sits behind a bamboo paywall card with a 'spend 12 stalks to read' button that unlocks the body in place. A topic filter rail (Bamboo, Naps, Tall Trees, Being Left Alone) narrows the feed client-side.

design_direction.style: Warm literary-review magazine, light print mode. A Sunday book-supplement layout on warm cream stock: an oversized Libre Bodoni masthead and headlines do the heavy lifting, with ruled columns, drop caps opening each section, and Bodoni-italic pull quotes set in the margins. One chestnut rust carries the masthead rules, brand, section kickers, and bylines; one rationed bamboo green is reserved for the single primary CTA, the topic tags, and member badges, and appears almost nowhere else. Source Serif 4 carries calm long-measure body copy with old-style numerals; figures that pretend to be data (prices, nap counts, bamboo balances) stay in the serif with tabular figures rather than a console monospace, on purpose, to read like a periodical and not a dashboard. Hairline rust rules between sections, generous 8px-rhythm whitespace, deliberate column measure around 66 characters. No gradients beyond a faint paper vignette, no glassmorphism, no neon, no drop-shadow marketing cards, no monospace telemetry. Reads like a respected literary quarterly that has been handed, with complete sincerity, to a solitary animal that sleeps eighteen hours a day.
design_direction.archetype: Editorial / longform publishing landing page (masthead, contents, pull quotes, ruled columns)
design_direction.motion_vocab: Quiet print-supplement motion: drop caps and section rules fade in on scroll, the primary CTA gives a soft ink-fill on hover, pull quotes settle gently. No parallax, no slides, respects reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Full-width chestnut masthead rule over an oversized Bodoni headline set as a magazine cover line, an issue-number kicker ('Vol. I, No. 1 / The Canopy Review'), a single bamboo-green CTA, and a lead paragraph opening with a large drop cap. No hero photograph, the type is the image.

images.rationale: Typographic editorial style, so the hero photograph was skipped (the masthead type is the image). Demo is a reading feed whose author avatars are headshot-shaped, and the landing page has three named testimonials, so the full budget went to three deadpan red-panda author portraits reused as both testimonial headshots and demo author avatars. No demo screen asset beyond avatars was needed.

items.prompt: Editorial author portrait of a red panda at a desk with a leather book, three-quarter angle looking off camera, warm cream backdrop, chestnut and bamboo-green tones, magazine portraiture, no text.
items.verdict: pass: warm author-at-desk portrait, palette harmonizes, no text artifacts

items.prompt: Editorial author portrait of a red panda facing camera with a deadpan unimpressed expression, centered, warm cream backdrop, chestnut fur, magazine portraiture, no text.
items.verdict: pass: ideal deadpan direct-gaze headshot on cream, clean

items.prompt: Editorial author portrait of a red panda in profile gazing into the middle distance, contemplative sleepy air, warm cream backdrop, chestnut and faint bamboo-green tones, magazine portraiture, no text.
items.verdict: pass: contemplative profile, on-palette, no text
